message:
fix(tui): fix zM (collapse-all) to discover deeply nested foldable nodes

tree_collapse_all() previously iterated only the currently-visible
tree_flat_nodes to find nodes with children, then inserted their IDs
into tree_collapsed. This missed deeply nested foldable nodes that were
hidden inside already-collapsed parents — collapsing the visible parents
first meant their hidden children never appeared in the flat node list
and thus were never marked as collapsed. Re-expanding a parent would
then reveal its children still in an expanded state.

Fix by first clearing all collapsed state and rebuilding the flat node
list (so every node at every depth is visible), collecting all foldable
node IDs, then inserting them all into tree_collapsed and rebuilding
again. The double rebuild is necessary: the first reveals all nodes, and
the second applies the fully-collapsed view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
